items.title: Setup mentions a GUI step: 'log in to the dashboard'
items.rationale: Setup mentions a GUI step: 'log in to the dashboard'.
items.fix_hint:
Replace "open the dashboard to copy your API key" with "set
$YOURAPP_API_KEY (get one at https://example.com/settings/api)".
Move any GUI step into a one-time human bootstrap section clearly
separated from the headless install / run flow.

items.title: Repository has no commits (not a git repo or empty)
items.rationale: Repository has no commits (not a git repo or empty).
items.fix_hint:
If you imported the project as a single squash, re-import preserving
history (`git filter-repo` or a fresh clone of the source). For new
projects, just keep committing — agents need the trail.

items.title: Setup section has 7 distinct commands
items.rationale: Setup section has 7 distinct commands — consider wrapping in `make install` or a setup script.
items.fix_hint:
Bundle multi-step setup into a single `make setup` (or
`scripts/setup`) target. The README then documents one command
instead of five. The script can still do five things — it just
hides the noise from the agent.

items.title: Repository has 4 commits
items.rationale: Repository has 4 commits — fewer than min_commits=5.
items.fix_hint:
If you imported the project as a single squash, re-import preserving
history (`git filter-repo` or a fresh clone of the source). For new
projects, just keep committing — agents need the trail.

items.title: At least one entry-point convention is present
items.rationale: At least one entry-point convention is present. (no clause matched)
items.fix_hint:
Add a `[project.scripts]` table to pyproject.toml mapping a CLI
name to your `module.cli:main` entry point. For Go, add
`cmd/<name>/main.go`. For Node, set `"bin"` or `"main"` in
package.json. The goal: an agent running `--help` on the package
should find a command that works.

items.title: Setup mentions a GUI step: 'log in to the dashboard'
items.rationale: Setup mentions a GUI step: 'log in to the dashboard'.
items.fix_hint:
Replace "open the dashboard to copy your API key" with "set
$YOURAPP_API_KEY (get one at https://example.com/settings/api)".
Move any GUI step into a one-time human bootstrap section clearly
separated from the headless install / run flow.

items.title: At least one ecosystem-specific lockfile is committed
items.rationale: At least one ecosystem-specific lockfile is committed. (no clause matched)
items.fix_hint:
Run `poetry lock` / `npm install` / `cargo update` (whichever your
manifest needs) and commit the resulting lockfile. For pip-only
Python projects, freezing `requirements.txt` to exact versions or
switching to `uv` / `poetry` is acceptable.

items.title: No project manifest found (pyproject
items.rationale: No project manifest found (pyproject.toml, package.json, Cargo.toml, go.mod, ...).
items.fix_hint:
Drop a `pyproject.toml` (or `package.json` / `Cargo.toml` / etc.)
at the repo root with at least name + version + dependencies. For
monorepos, every package directory should have its own manifest.

items.title: Hotspot: src/agent_readiness/cli
items.rationale: Hotspot: src/agent_readiness/cli.py changed 10 times and is >200 lines.
items.fix_hint:
For each hotspot, identify the dominant axes of change (e.g. one
file holds both API routing and validation logic) and split along
those lines. After the split, both halves see lower per-file churn.

items.title: README is missing signals for: install
items.rationale: README is missing signals for: install.
items.fix_hint:
Add three short sections to README: Install (one fenced block),
Run (one fenced block showing the canonical entry point), Test
(one fenced block). Three fenced blocks is the floor; if your
project genuinely needs more, write more.

items.title: README references pytest but no pytest configuration was found
items.rationale: README references pytest but no pytest configuration was found.
items.fix_hint:
If your README says "run `pytest`", drop a `[tool.pytest.ini_options]`
table in pyproject.toml so a fresh checkout actually runs tests
with `pytest`. Same logic for any other tool you mention. Agent
docs should be ≥ 100 bytes of real content, not a stub.

items.title: Large file: public/data/tenants/agent-readiness-project/overview
items.rationale: Large file: public/data/tenants/agent-readiness-project/overview.json (565 lines > 500)
items.fix_hint:
Split the file into smaller modules grouped by concept, or add an
exclusion in `.agent-readiness.toml` if the file is truly intentional
(large generated tables, etc.).

items.title: None of these expected paths exist:
items.rationale: None of these expected paths exist: .coveragerc, codecov.yml, .codecov.yml, coveralls.yml, .coveralls.yml
items.fix_hint:
Add a coverage config (`.coveragerc` for pytest-cov, `codecov.yml` for
Codecov) so coverage signal is visible to both humans and agents.

items.title: At least one reproducible-workspace config is present
items.rationale: At least one reproducible-workspace config is present. (no clause matched)
items.fix_hint:
Drop a minimal `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` referencing a
Microsoft base image plus `postCreateCommand: pip install -e .`.
For Nix shops, `flake.nix` with a dev shell is the equivalent.

items.title: Branch Rulesets: Configured
items.rationale: `gh api repos/harrydaihaolin/agent-readiness-analytics-dashboard/rulesets` returned an empty result for harrydaihaolin/agent-readiness-analytics-dashboard.
items.fix_hint:
In repo Settings → Branches (or the new Rules / Rulesets UI),
require: pull request before merging, status checks (your CI)
passing, branch up-to-date. Apply to `main` and any release
branches.

items.title: Issue templates, PR template, or CODEOWNERS configured
items.rationale: Issue templates, PR template, or CODEOWNERS configured. (no clause matched)
items.fix_hint:
At minimum: drop `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md` (GitHub has
a wizard), a `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` with a checklist,
and a `CODEOWNERS` file mapping `*` to the team that should review
agent-opened PRs.
